title: Assessment: Broaden Specification Beyond Single Examples

questions.question: When is parametrization the most natural next move?

options.A: When one behavior clearly has a family of concrete cases
options.B: When you want to avoid naming behavior explicitly
options.C: When every test should become property-based immediately

questions.explanation: Parametrization broadens one behavior into several explicit cases without duplicating the test body.

questions.question: What is the defining value of a property-based test in this course?

options.B: It checks an invariant across many generated inputs and can reveal falsifying examples
options.D: It only matters for math libraries

questions.explanation: Property-based testing broadens the specification by checking invariants and surfacing concrete counterexamples.

questions.question: What is the main organizing principle in the course's advanced coverage model?

options.B: Choose layers by the kind of truth you need from each boundary
options.D: Keep only unit tests and cards

questions.explanation: The module synthesizes coverage choices around learning goals such as logic, invariants, lifecycle, and boundary truth.

questions.question: Explain why property-based testing complements rather than replaces example-driven TDD.

required_points: Small concrete examples still help define the intended behavior clearly
Properties broaden the specification once the invariant is understood
Generated tests search for surprising counterexamples that hand-picked examples may miss
answer_key.full_credit: Shows the progression from explicit examples to broader invariants without collapsing them into one technique
answer_key.partial_credit: Mentions examples and properties but not their complementary roles

questions.prompt: You are testing a component that validates inputs, writes to a database, and calls an external service. Use Module 6 to justify a layered suite that includes some combination of example tests, parametrization, properties, persistence realism, and boundary verification.
questions.guidance: Strong answers justify each layer by the question it answers rather than by attachment to a favorite testing style.

rubric.A: Builds a layered suite where each test type earns its place by answering a distinct question
rubric.B: Uses several layers but with partial overlap or weak justification
rubric.C: Mostly lists tools without explaining why each one belongs
